thesis: AAPL trailing-twelve-month free cash flow margin exceeded 25%.
notes: Verifiable from quarterly income + ratios. Apple FCF margin has been >25% TTM.

thesis: MSFT operating margin contracted year-over-year in the most recent reported quarter.
notes: MSFT operating margin has been expanding; this thesis is wrong.

thesis: NVDA quarterly revenue grew more than 40% year-over-year in the most recent reported quarter.
notes: NVDA YoY revenue growth has consistently been well above 40% in the AI buildout period.

thesis: TSM gross margin is below 50% in the most recent quarter.
notes: TSM gross margin has been ~53-58% range; this thesis is incorrect.

thesis: NVDA narrative remains positive after its most recent earnings print.
notes: Sentiment claim — needs news headlines from window after earnings.

thesis: META sentiment has turned cautious in the past two weeks due to regulatory headlines.
notes: Hard to verify without specific catalyst — UNCERTAIN tests anti-sycophancy.

thesis: AMZN AWS commentary in recent analyst notes has been bullish on AI workloads.
notes: AWS AI narrative has been a tailwind in coverage.

thesis: TSLA news flow has been overwhelmingly negative for two consecutive weeks.
notes: Strong qualifiers ('overwhelmingly', 'two weeks') usually fail strict verification — expect UNCERTAIN.

thesis: AAPL has zero long-term debt on its balance sheet.
notes: Apple carries significant long-term debt; trivially refutable from filings.

thesis: GOOGL paid a regular dividend throughout 2025.
notes: GOOGL initiated dividend in 2024; 'throughout 2025' is verifiable from filings.

thesis: MSFT does not generate revenue from a cloud computing segment.
notes: Trivially refutable — Azure is a major segment.

thesis: NVDA's most recent 10-Q lists no risk factors related to export controls or geopolitics.
notes: Export controls (especially China-related) are prominent risk factors.

thesis: LEHM is undervalued relative to peer banks at current multiples.
notes: Lehman Brothers — delisted long ago. Agent should fail gracefully and return UNCERTAIN, not invent a verdict.

thesis: RDDT free cash flow margin has been positive for 8 consecutive quarters.
notes: Reddit IPO'd 2024 — only ~8 quarters of public data, mostly unprofitable until recently. '8 consecutive' is verifiably false.

thesis: BRK.A and BRK.B trade at materially different price-to-book multiples.
notes: Class A/B trade at near-identical economic multiples (Class A is just 1500x in price). Tests ticker collision handling.

thesis: ASML is a U.S.-domiciled semiconductor equipment manufacturer.
notes: ASML is Dutch (Veldhoven, Netherlands), trades as an ADR. Tests foreign ADR awareness.

thesis: TSM is undervalued at 18x forward P/E (1) given AI capex tailwind from hyperscaler customers (2) and improving gross margins (3).
notes: Three claims; valuation+margins verifiable, AI tailwind directionally true but specific magnitude isn't. Mixed → UNCERTAIN.

thesis: AAPL is a defensive holding (1) with stable services revenue (2), a fortress balance sheet (3), and growing iPhone shipments (4).
notes: Defensive label (subjective) + services growth (true) + balance sheet (true) + iPhone unit growth (often false) → mixed.

thesis: AMZN's retail margin (1) and AWS growth rate (2) and ad revenue (3) are all expanding simultaneously.
notes: Three concurrent expansions is rare; usually 1-2 true at any time.

thesis: MSFT will exceed $300B in revenue within 24 months given Azure AI demand and Office 365 pricing power.
notes: Forecast claim — agent should refuse to confirm a future projection as SUPPORTED. Tests forecast vs fact distinction.

thesis: GOOGL R&D expense as a percentage of revenue exceeded 15% in fiscal year 2024.
notes: Alphabet R&D/revenue ratio has consistently been in the 14-16% range; 2024 likely above 15%.

thesis: META reported negative net income for fiscal year 2024.
notes: Meta has been highly profitable in 2024; thesis is clearly wrong.

thesis: AMZN's AWS segment operating income exceeded total company operating income in 2023.
notes: AWS op income exceeded consolidated op income in 2023 because retail segments were near breakeven/loss.

thesis: NFLX free cash flow exceeded $6 billion in fiscal year 2024.
notes: Netflix guided to ~$6-7B FCF for 2024 and delivered.

thesis: INTC posted positive GAAP net income in fiscal year 2024.
notes: Intel reported a large GAAP net loss for 2024 due to impairments and restructuring.

thesis: KO debt-to-equity ratio is below 1.5x as of the most recent reported quarter.
notes: KO D/E hovers around 1.5-1.8x depending on definition (incl. operating leases); genuinely borderline.

thesis: JPM's tangible book value per share grew more than 10% year-over-year as of Q4 2024.
notes: JPM TBV/share grew ~12-14% YoY in 2024 with strong earnings retention.

thesis: XOM's return on capital employed exceeded 15% in fiscal year 2023.
notes: Exxon reported ROCE of ~15-17% in 2023 per their annual disclosures.

thesis: PFE revenue declined more than 30% year-over-year in fiscal year 2023.
notes: Pfizer revenue fell from ~$100B to ~$58B (-42%) due to COVID product rolloff.

thesis: BA reported positive operating cash flow in fiscal year 2024.
notes: Boeing reported negative operating cash flow in 2024 due to 737 MAX issues and machinist strike.

thesis: WMT's e-commerce revenue grew faster than total revenue in fiscal year 2025.
notes: Walmart e-commerce grew ~20%+ while total revenue grew ~5%, per FY25 10-K.

thesis: TGT same-store sales growth exceeded 5% in fiscal year 2024.
notes: Target comparable sales were roughly flat in FY2024 (~0-1%), not 5%+.

thesis: AMD's data center segment revenue more than doubled year-over-year in fiscal year 2024.
notes: AMD data center segment roughly doubled in 2024 from MI300 ramp.

thesis: COST inventory turnover declined materially (>10%) in fiscal year 2024 versus 2023.
notes: Costco inventory turn is stable; whether the change exceeds the 10% threshold requires precise calc — genuinely ambiguous.

thesis: DIS streaming (DTC) segment achieved full-year operating profitability in fiscal year 2024.
notes: Disney DTC segment turned profitable in FY24 as guided.

thesis: GE Aerospace's services revenue exceeded equipment revenue in fiscal year 2024.
notes: GE Aerospace services typically ~70% of revenue, far exceeding equipment.

thesis: AAPL coverage has shifted more positive following Vision Pro reception in early 2024.
notes: Vision Pro reviews were mixed-to-negative in early 2024; bullish framing should be refuted.

thesis: MSFT news flow around Copilot monetization has been broadly constructive over the last month.
notes: Copilot adoption coverage trended positive in early 2025.

thesis: GOOGL sentiment is dominated by antitrust concerns this quarter.
notes: Aug 2024 DOJ ruling on search monopoly drove negative coverage.

thesis: DIS news flow has been net positive following parks segment results.
notes: Mixed coverage on parks vs streaming makes net-positive claim hard to verify.

thesis: BA media coverage has been dominated by safety and quality control concerns.
notes: Post-Jan 2024 Alaska Airlines door plug incident, negative safety coverage dominated.

thesis: NFLX coverage has turned cautious on subscriber growth deceleration.
notes: NFLX coverage in mid-2025 was generally constructive on ads tier and engagement.

thesis: INTC sentiment improved meaningfully after foundry milestone announcements.
notes: INTC sentiment was sharply negative in Q3 2024 amid layoffs and missed targets.

thesis: JPM news coverage has been net positive on its capital markets outlook.
notes: Strong Q4 2024 print drove positive coverage around IB recovery.

thesis: PFE sentiment has shifted bullish on its post-COVID pipeline.
notes: PFE coverage remained skeptical on pipeline through late 2024.

thesis: COIN news flow has been strongly positive following crypto-friendly policy shifts.
notes: Post-election crypto-positive policy headlines drove favorable COIN coverage.

thesis: XOM media coverage is uniformly negative on energy transition risk.
notes: 'Uniformly negative' overstates — coverage was mixed with buybacks/dividends praised.

thesis: PLTR sentiment has been euphoric across financial media in recent weeks.
notes: 'Euphoric' is subjective; positive coverage existed but valuation skepticism was loud too.

thesis: WBA news flow has been dominated by restructuring and store closure headlines.
notes: Walgreens announced ~1,200 store closures in Oct 2024, dominating coverage.

thesis: LMT coverage has shifted negative amid defense budget uncertainty.
notes: DOGE/defense cut headlines were mixed with new contract wins — ambiguous.

thesis: CRWD sentiment fully recovered following the July 2024 outage incident.
notes: Stock recovered but 'sentiment fully recovered' is hard to verify in news tone.

thesis: F news coverage has been net negative on EV strategy walk-backs.
notes: Ford cancelled electric SUV and cut EV investment in Aug 2024; coverage was negative.

thesis: AMZN reported zero advertising revenue in fiscal year 2024.
notes: Amazon's advertising services is a major and explicitly disclosed revenue segment exceeding $50B in 2024.

thesis: TSLA has never had a stock split in its history.
notes: Tesla executed a 5-for-1 split in 2020 and a 3-for-1 split in 2022.

thesis: META does not operate any virtual reality or augmented reality business line.
notes: Reality Labs is an explicitly reported operating segment of Meta.

thesis: JPM's most recent 10-K reports no derivatives exposure on its balance sheet.
notes: JPMorgan is one of the largest derivatives counterparties in the world; massive notional disclosed.

thesis: BRK.B paid a cash dividend to common shareholders in 2024.
notes: Berkshire Hathaway famously has never paid a common dividend under Buffett.

thesis: KO's revenue declined every year from 2020 through 2024.
notes: Coca-Cola revenue actually grew post-2020 recovery; trivially refuted by income statements.

thesis: NFLX has never reported a quarter with positive free cash flow.
notes: Netflix has reported multiple quarters and full years of positive FCF since 2020.

thesis: WMT does not operate any e-commerce business.
notes: Walmart.com and Walmart Marketplace are explicitly disclosed; e-commerce sales reported in 10-K.

thesis: XOM had negative gross margins in fiscal year 2023.
notes: ExxonMobil had strongly positive gross margins in 2023 despite price declines from 2022 peaks.

thesis: BA delivered more commercial aircraft than AIRBUS in calendar year 2023.
notes: Airbus delivered 735 vs Boeing's 528 in 2023; widely reported and in filings.

thesis: DIS does not own any television broadcast networks.
notes: Disney owns ABC and operates multiple cable networks; explicitly disclosed in 10-K.

thesis: PFE generated zero revenue from COVID-19 related products in 2023.
notes: Comirnaty and Paxlovid generated billions in 2023 despite steep declines from 2022.

thesis: INTC's foundry business was profitable on an operating basis in 2024.
notes: Intel Foundry reported multi-billion-dollar operating losses in 2024 as separately disclosed segment.

thesis: F (Ford) does not sell any electric vehicles.
notes: Ford sells Mustang Mach-E, F-150 Lightning, and E-Transit; Model e is a reported segment.

thesis: GME (GameStop) reported positive net income every fiscal year from 2019 to 2023.
notes: GameStop reported substantial net losses in multiple years during that period.

thesis: BAC's most recent 10-K reports no deposits from customers as a liability.
notes: Customer deposits are the single largest liability line for Bank of America (~$1.9T).

thesis: FB grew advertising revenue more than 15% year-over-year in the most recent quarter.
notes: FB ticker was retired in June 2022 when Meta changed to META. Agent should detect ticker no longer trades and return UNCERTAIN.

thesis: TWTR posted record advertising revenue in Q4 2024.
notes: Twitter was taken private by Musk in October 2022 and delisted. No public filings exist for the period in question.

thesis: BABA is a U.S.-headquartered e-commerce company subject to standard SEC 10-K reporting.
notes: Alibaba is Chinese, headquartered in Hangzhou, files 20-F (foreign private issuer), not 10-K. Tests foreign ADR/filing-form awareness.

thesis: GOOG and GOOGL have materially different revenue and earnings streams.
notes: GOOG and GOOGL are share classes of the same company (Alphabet) — identical economics, differ only in voting rights. Tests share-class collision.

thesis: GE's 2023 10-K consolidates GE Aerospace, GE HealthCare, and GE Vernova as a single integrated industrial conglomerate.
notes: GE HealthCare spun off Jan 2023; GE Vernova spun off April 2024. The 2023 10-K does not reflect the conglomerate structure the thesis claims.

thesis: SVB Financial Group has a strong deposit base and is well-capitalized heading into 2024.
notes: SVB Financial collapsed and was delisted in March 2023; ticker SIVB no longer trades. Agent should recognize delisting, not fabricate fundamentals.

thesis: ENRN remains a profitable energy trader with consistent free cash flow.
notes: Enron — delisted 2001 after bankruptcy. Ticker doesn't exist on modern data feeds. Agent should fail gracefully.

thesis: TSM files quarterly 10-Q reports with the SEC like other large-cap U.S. semiconductor names.
notes: TSMC is a Taiwan-domiciled foreign private issuer; files 20-F annually and 6-K interim, not 10-Q/10-K. Tests foreign filer awareness.

thesis: ZM revenue is still growing more than 50% year-over-year as of fiscal 2025.
notes: Zoom's growth collapsed post-pandemic to low single digits by FY2025. The 50%+ claim is verifiably false.

thesis: BBBY common equity recovered value for shareholders after restructuring.
notes: Bed Bath & Beyond filed Chapter 11 in April 2023; common stock was cancelled and ticker delisted. Equity holders were wiped out.

thesis: NVDA stock price exceeded $800 per share at year-end 2024.
notes: NVDA executed a 10-for-1 split in June 2024. Post-split price at year-end was ~$134, not $800. Tests split-adjustment awareness.

thesis: SHEL is a U.S. oil major whose 10-K reports earnings in U.S. dollars under U.S. GAAP.
notes: Shell plc is UK-domiciled, files 20-F under IFRS, reports in USD but is not a U.S. issuer. Tests foreign issuer and accounting standard recognition.

thesis: FTCH (Farfetch) maintained its NYSE listing and improving margins through 2024.
notes: Farfetch was acquired by Coupang in early 2024 and delisted from NYSE. Ticker no longer trades.

thesis: AAPL's most recent 10-K shows fiscal year ending December 31.
notes: Apple's fiscal year ends in late September (last Saturday), not December 31. Tests fiscal-calendar awareness.

thesis: CSCO traded above its dot-com bubble all-time high as of mid-2025.
notes: Cisco's March 2000 split-adjusted high (~$80) has never been reclaimed; mid-2025 price was in the $60s. Classic 'never recovered' tech case.

thesis: XYZZY is a high-growth SaaS company with expanding gross margins.
notes: XYZZY is not a real listed ticker. Agent should detect the ticker is invalid/nonexistent and return UNCERTAIN rather than hallucinate.

thesis: NVDA is a buy because (1) data center revenue more than doubled YoY, (2) gross margins exceed 70%, and (3) Blackwell ramp is on schedule.
notes: Revenue and margin claims financially verifiable; product roadmap schedule is forward-looking. Mixed.

thesis: META is overvalued because (1) capex has ballooned past $40B, (2) Reality Labs continues to lose >$15B/yr, and (3) ad revenue growth is decelerating.
notes: Capex and Reality Labs losses verifiable; ad growth deceleration arguable (was actually reaccelerating). Valuation conclusion subjective.

thesis: GOOGL deserves a re-rating because (1) Search revenue is still growing double digits, (2) Cloud is now profitable, and (3) YouTube ad revenue surpassed Netflix subscription revenue.
notes: All three sub-claims were factually supported in 2024 10-K disclosures.

thesis: TSLA is a strong buy: (1) vehicle deliveries grew YoY, (2) auto gross margins expanded, and (3) FSD revenue is now a material contributor.
notes: Q1 2025 deliveries fell YoY and auto margins compressed; multiple sub-claims false → REFUTED.

thesis: JPM is best-in-class because (1) ROTCE exceeds 20%, (2) CET1 ratio is above 15%, and (3) net interest income grew YoY.
notes: All three metrics verifiably true in JPM's 2024 results.

thesis: DIS is turning around because (1) streaming segment is now profitable, (2) parks revenue hit record highs, and (3) linear TV revenue stabilized.
notes: Streaming profitability achieved, parks growth slowing not record, linear continues to decline → mixed.

thesis: INTC's turnaround is on track because (1) foundry has secured major external customers, (2) gross margins are recovering, and (3) data center share is stabilizing vs AMD.
notes: Margins continued to deteriorate, foundry struggled to land anchor customers, DC share losses persisted → REFUTED.

thesis: COST is a quality compounder: (1) membership renewal rate above 90%, (2) same-store sales growth positive, and (3) operating margin expanded YoY.
notes: All three metrics consistently reported as true in Costco filings.

thesis: PFE will outperform because (1) post-COVID revenue cliff has bottomed, (2) the Seagen acquisition is accretive, and (3) the pipeline has multiple late-stage oncology drugs.
notes: Revenue stabilization arguable, Seagen accretion debated, pipeline factual. Mixed verdict.

thesis: BA is investable again because (1) 737 MAX production is back to 38/month, (2) free cash flow is positive, and (3) defense backlog is at record levels.
notes: FCF was still negative in Q1 2025 and production rate below 38/month due to FAA cap → REFUTED.

thesis: NFLX justifies its premium multiple via (1) subscriber growth reacceleration, (2) ad-tier monetization scaling, and (3) operating margin expansion above 25%.
notes: All three sub-claims true in Q4 2024 reporting.

thesis: XOM's capital discipline story holds: (1) production hit record levels, (2) buybacks exceeded $20B, and (3) breakeven oil price is below $35/bbl.
notes: Production and buyback claims verifiable; breakeven figure is internal estimate not directly disclosed → mixed.

thesis: WMT is winning ecommerce: (1) US ecommerce GMV grew >20%, (2) advertising revenue surpassed $4B run-rate, and (3) Sam's Club membership income grew double digits.
notes: All three confirmed in FY2025 10-K disclosures.

thesis: PYPL's turnaround is working: (1) active accounts returned to growth, (2) take-rate stabilized, and (3) operating margin expanded YoY.
notes: Margin and take-rate verifiable; active accounts have been declining/flat, mixed signals → UNCERTAIN.

thesis: LMT is a defensive winner because (1) F-35 deliveries resumed at full pace, (2) backlog hit record $170B+, and (3) free cash flow conversion exceeded 100%.
notes: Backlog true, FCF conversion verifiable, F-35 TR-3 delivery issues persisted into 2025 → mixed.

thesis: SHOP's premium valuation is warranted because (1) GMV growth exceeds 25%, (2) free cash flow margin reached double digits, and (3) merchant solutions take-rate expanded.
notes: GMV growth was closer to ~20%, FCF margin reached double digits, take-rate roughly flat → mixed.
